mnemonics: E-WASTE ELITE: E-Waste Always Starts Today, Ending Landfills Is The Elite goal.
Remember the 3 R's for E-Waste: Reduce, Reuse, Recycle (but think of a 4th: Responsibility).
keyPoints: E-Waste contains hazardous materials (lead, mercury) that can leach into soil and water.
Proper recycling recovers valuable materials like gold, silver, and copper.
The informal sector's unsafe handling practices pose serious health and environmental risks.
Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) shifts the disposal burden back to manufacturers.
Landfilling e-waste is the least desirable option due to long-term contamination risks.
commonMistakes: Assuming all e-waste is the same; different electronics have different hazardous material levels and recycling processes.
Believing simply deleting data makes a device safe for disposal; physical destruction of storage drives is often required.
Confusing 'donating' old electronics with responsible recycling; non-functional donated items often become e-waste for the recipient.
